basics.title: Routing Basics
basics.description: Next.js uses a file-system based router. Every folder inside app/ becomes a URL segment, and page.tsx makes it publicly accessible.
basics.navMethodsDesc: Next.js provides several ways to navigate between pages.
basics.linkDesc: The {'<'}Link{'>'} component extends HTML {'<'}a{'>'} with prefetching and client-side navigation.
basics.routerDesc: For programmatic navigation inside event handlers. Must be used in Client Components with 'use client'.
basics.redirectDesc: For server-side redirects inside Server Components, Server Actions, or Route Handlers.
basics.prefetchingDesc: Link automatically prefetches routes when they appear in the viewport. Static routes are fully prefetched; dynamic routes prefetch up to the nearest loading.tsx boundary.
basics.softHardDesc: Client-side navigation (Link, useRouter) is "soft" — only changed segments re-render. Full page reload is "hard" — the entire page re-renders from scratch.

dynamic.title: Dynamic Routes
dynamic.description: Dynamic segments let you create pages from dynamic data. The [slug] folder name becomes a route parameter.
dynamic.tryChanging: Try changing the URL! Replace the last segment with anything.
dynamic.howItWorksDesc: In Next.js 16, params is a Promise and must be awaited. This is a breaking change from earlier versions.
dynamic.paramsPromiseDesc: In Next.js 16, params must be awaited in Server Components. In Client Components, use React.use(params) instead.
dynamic.generateStaticDesc: Use generateStaticParams() to pre-render dynamic routes at build time (SSG). Unknown slugs are rendered on-demand (SSR) by default.

catchAll.title: Catch-all Segments
catchAll.description: [...slug] matches any number of URL segments. The parameter becomes a string array.
catchAll.docSitesDesc: /docs/getting-started/installation — nested doc pages with dynamic depth.
catchAll.blogDatesDesc: /blog/2024/04/16/my-post — date-based URL structure parsed from segments.

groups.title: Route Groups
groups.description: Route groups organize routes without affecting the URL structure. Wrap a folder name in parentheses (folderName) to create a group.
groups.liveExampleDesc: This learning hub uses two route groups:
groups.learnGroup: → All demo pages with sidebar layout (this page!)
groups.urlNote: The URL for this page is
groups.urlNoteEnd: — notice (learn) is NOT in the URL!
groups.multipleLayoutsDesc: Each route group can have its own layout. This is perfect for sections with different UI shells.
groups.urlNotAffectedDesc: (marketing)/about/page.tsx renders at /about, not /(marketing)/about. The group folder is purely organizational.
groups.separateLayoutsDesc: Each group can have its own layout.tsx. Great for sections with different UI shells (e.g., marketing site vs dashboard).
groups.noDuplicatesDesc: Routes across groups cannot resolve to the same URL. (marketing)/about and (shop)/about would cause a build error.
groups.fullReloadDesc: Navigating between groups with different root layouts triggers a full page reload (not a soft navigation).

parallel.title: Parallel Routes
parallel.description: Parallel routes render multiple pages simultaneously in the same layout using @named slots. Each slot can have independent loading and error states.
parallel.defaultRequiredDesc: On hard navigation (page refresh), Next.js needs a default for unmatched slots. Without it, you get a 404.
parallel.independentLoadingDesc: Each slot can have its own loading.tsx and error.tsx, so one slow slot won't block the others.
parallel.conditionalRenderDesc: You can conditionally render slots based on auth state, user roles, or any server-side condition.
parallel.softHardNavDesc: Soft navigation preserves slot state. Hard navigation (refresh) resets to default.tsx content.
parallel.mainContentDesc: This is the children slot — the default page.tsx content. The layout renders this alongside the @analytics and @dashboard slots simultaneously.

intercepting.title: Intercepting Routes
intercepting.description: Intercepting routes let you load a route within the current layout — perfect for modals that show content without losing page context.
intercepting.softNavDesc: Clicking a Link → route is intercepted → content loads in a modal overlay → URL updates but page stays
intercepting.hardNavDesc: Direct URL / page refresh → goes to the actual route → full page renders normally
intercepting.photoDemoDesc: Click a photo to see the detail page. In a full implementation, clicking would show a modal (intercepted), while direct URL access shows the full page.
intercepting.requiresParallelDesc: The modal pattern needs a @modal parallel route slot in the layout. The intercepted route goes inside @modal/(.)path.
intercepting.urlUpdatesDesc: The URL updates to the intercepted route (shareable!), but the content loads in-place without a full page navigation.
intercepting.commonUseCasesDesc: Photo galleries, login modals, shopping cart previews, social media post details — anything that benefits from an overlay.
intercepting.refreshFullPageDesc: Refreshing the page or sharing the URL loads the actual route (not the intercepted version). Best of both worlds.
intercepting.fullPageView: This is the FULL page view. In a real intercepting routes setup, clicking from the gallery would show a modal instead.
intercepting.navigationDesc: This page loaded as a full page (not intercepted). Try refreshing — you'll always see this full view, not a modal.

notFound.title: Error Pages
notFound.description: Next.js provides file conventions for handling errors gracefully — not-found.tsx, error.tsx, forbidden.tsx, and unauthorized.tsx.
notFound.interactiveDemoDesc: Click the buttons to trigger different error types. Each is handled by its own file convention.
notFound.triggerDesc: Each button navigates to a dynamic route that either throws an error or calls notFound(). The error is caught by the corresponding file convention.
notFound.notFoundDesc: Rendered when notFound() is called or when no route matches.
notFound.errorDesc: Must be a Client Component. Catches runtime errors and shows a recovery UI.
notFound.forbiddenDesc: Handle 403 and 401 responses. Requires enabling authInterrupts in next.config.ts.
notFound.errorMustBeClientDesc: error.tsx requires "use client" because it uses React's Error Boundary mechanism, which only works on the client.
notFound.nestingMattersDesc: error.tsx catches errors from its child page.tsx, NOT from its sibling layout.tsx. Use global-error.tsx for root layout errors.
notFound.loadingTsxDesc: loading.tsx creates a Suspense boundary around page.tsx. It shows instantly on navigation while the page loads.
notFound.errorDigestDesc: In production, error.digest is a hash of the error (not the full message) to avoid leaking sensitive info to the client.
notFound.pageNotFoundDesc: This is a custom not-found.tsx — it renders when notFound() is called.
notFound.normalPageDesc: This page loaded successfully because the type "{type}" didn't trigger any errors.
